metaDescription: A 16-item pre-suit checklist for contract disputes: notice and cure provisions, ADR escalation, forum selection, limitations periods, and the keep-performing question.
summary: A practical 16-item pre-suit checklist for in-house counsel and litigators handling a commercial contract dispute, covering the conditions precedent, clauses, performance decisions, and records that determine how the case can be fought.
keywords: forum selection and choice of law clause
whether to keep performing after breach
assembling the operative contract for litigation

faq.question: What should you do before filing a lawsuit over a contract dispute?
faq.answer: Work through the conditions precedent first: comply with the notice clause to the letter, run the full cure period before terminating, follow any escalation ladder (executive conference, then mediation, then arbitration or suit) with waiting periods documented, and check for short industry-specific deadlines like construction lien and bond deadlines, UCC notice-of-breach windows, and government-contract claim procedures. This is general information, not legal advice.

faq.question: Can a contract shorten the statute of limitations for a breach claim?
faq.answer: Yes — contracts can shorten limitations periods and courts often enforce the shortened ones; for example, the UCC's four-year default can be reduced to one year. Calculate the deadline under every plausible reading and calendar the earliest. Laws vary by jurisdiction; this is not legal advice.

faq.question: Is it safe to stop performing a contract when the other side breaches?
faq.answer: Stopping is only safe if the other side's breach is material — guess wrong and you become the breaching party. This single decision deserves a counsel conversation more than anything else on the checklist.

faq.question: What is a demand for adequate assurance under the UCC?
faq.answer: In goods cases, UCC section 2-609 lets you demand assurance in writing when reasonable grounds for insecurity arise, and suspend your own performance while you wait. The page describes it as a powerful but underused way to force clarity without breaching.

faq.question: Why does following a contract's notice and cure provisions matter so precisely?
faq.answer: Courts enforce notice clauses mechanically — method, recipient, and content all matter, and "they obviously knew" is not compliance. You must also run the cure period fully before terminating; terminating early, such as on day 22 of a 30-day cure period, can hand the other side the breach claim.

faq.question: What records should you gather before a contract lawsuit?
faq.answer: Assemble the entire operative contract — master agreement, amendments, statements of work, purchase orders, change orders, and any unresolved terms-and-conditions — because disputes have been decided by which version was operative. Capture course of dealing early (invoices, delivery records, accommodations) since it lives in inboxes and ERP systems that rotate, and run the standard trio: litigation hold, notice to insurers and indemnitors, and privileged fact-gathering.
